description: The Non-Conformance Report (ISO 8.7 control of nonconforming outputs) raised when a received lot fails QC at receiving inspection or when damage/off-spec surfaces post-GRN. It identifies and segregates (quarantines) the affected goods, records the extent of non-conformance, and carries the disposition decision (return / rework / use-as-is-concession / scrap) that opens the downstream loop: return hands to RMA (diagram 11), a systemic cause opens a CorrectiveAction/SCAR (e04), and the result feeds supplier re-evaluation (8.4.1). The first node of the closed ISO loop 8.6 to 8.7 to 10.2 to 8.4.1.

fields.name: id
fields.label: NCR No.
fields.validation: system pattern; unique; immutable once raised
fields.purpose: Stable key carried as linkedNcrId onto the Return and as ncrId onto the CorrectiveAction, so the disposition, RMA and CAPA all trace to one non-conformance; the handle the ISO 8.7 record and an auditor reads.

fields.name: grnId
fields.label: Goods Receipt
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the NCR is raised against a received lot; may be null for a pre-GRN reject at inspection where no GRN was raised
fields.validation: resolves to a GRN of the same PO when set
fields.purpose: Links the non-conformance to the receipt it concerns, so commitment relief and the GR/IR accrual for the rejected quantity can be reversed on a return, and a post-GRN NCR ties back to its lot.

fields.name: inspectionId
fields.label: Inspection
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the NCR is raised from a receiving inspection (qcDecision = reject); null for a post-GRN NCR raised later
fields.validation: resolves to an Inspection with qcDecision = reject when set
fields.purpose: Traces the NCR to the inspection reject that raised it, carrying the test evidence and inspector forward; null when the NCR is raised post-GRN as damage/off-spec surfaces later.

fields.name: itemId
fields.label: Item
fields.validation: resolves to an item on the GRN/requisition line
fields.purpose: Names the nonconforming item so the disposition, the return line and the supplier quality history attribute the defect to the right material; the subject of the ISO 8.7 record.

fields.name: supplierId
fields.label: Supplier
fields.purpose: Names the supplier at fault so the NCR feeds the quality dimension of the scorecard and the re-evaluation; recurring NCRs auto-affect the composite and can trigger suspension (axiom A11).

fields.name: deliveryNoteNumber
fields.label: Delivery Note No.
fields.validation: non-empty; the supplier delivery-note reference for the affected consignment
fields.purpose: Pins the non-conformance to the exact consignment/delivery note so the supplier and the buyer can locate the shipment in dispute; an ISO 8.7 traceability field.

fields.name: deliveryDate
fields.label: Delivery Date
fields.validation: valid date; tenant timezone; display DD-MMM-YYYY
fields.purpose: The date the affected goods were delivered; dates the non-conformance for the supplier quality record and shows the detection lag when the NCR is raised post-GRN.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description of Non-Compliance
fields.purpose: States the nature of the defect (off-spec, damage, wrong item, expired) so the disposition decision and the supplier SCAR root-cause request have the factual basis; the core ISO 8.7 description.

fields.name: percentNonConformance
fields.label: % Non-Conformance
fields.purpose: Records the extent of the lot affected; sizes the disposition (partial return vs full scrap), is copied onto the return condition, and weights the supplier quality score.

fields.name: image
fields.label: Evidence Image
fields.mandatoryCondition: at least one image mandatory for defective / damaged / quality-fail dispositions
fields.purpose: Visual evidence of the defect retained on the ISO 8.7 record and carried to the supplier SCAR and the return; the proof that supports the claim against the supplier.

fields.name: raisedBy
fields.label: Raised By
fields.validation: a QC, Factory Manager, requester or Engineering user; from authenticated profile
fields.purpose: Records who raised the non-conformance (QC, Factory Manager, or requester/Engineering on the spares path) for accountability; NCRs are raisable by all three roles, not QC alone.

fields.name: disposition
fields.label: Disposition
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when the NCR moves from raised to dispositioned
fields.validation: one of the four; return hands to RMA (diagram 11); concession requires documented authority
fields.purpose: The decision on the nonconforming goods: return opens the RMA (S4), rework loops to re-inspection, concession documents acceptance with authority, scrap writes off (possible debit note); the branch that drives the rest of the loop (ISO 8.7).

fields.name: decidingAuthority
fields.label: Deciding Authority
fields.mandatoryCondition: set when a disposition is recorded
fields.validation: the authority who decided the disposition; retained per ISO 8.7
fields.purpose: Names who authorized the disposition (especially required for a use-as-is concession); the traceability-to-person ISO 8.7 mandates on every disposition record.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: raised on creation; dispositioned on disposition set; closed when disposition done AND (if a CAPA opened) effectiveness verified
fields.purpose: The NCR lifecycle position; an open NCR is one of the blocks that keeps the requisition out of COMPLETED (axiom A3), and it closes only when the disposition is executed and any CAPA effectiveness is verified.

invariants: A3 an open NCR/CAPA block prevents the requisition reaching COMPLETED
A11 closed ISO loop 8.6 -> 8.7 -> 10.2 -> 8.4.1; recurring NCRs auto-affect the supplier score; may suspend
A13 audit + SSE on every NCR change
A14 a quality NCR docks the four-factor perfect-order metric
goods are segregated/quarantined from raising until dispositioned (ISO 8.7)
disposition and deciding authority are always retained on the NCR (ISO 8.7 record)
